results.title: Keeping animals in the classroom
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Ivan Gaal and Curriculum Branch, Ministry of Education for Zoo Education Service
results.brief_description: Trevor Lunn introduces Karen Svalesen from the Zoo Education Service who examines the problems that can be encountered when keeping animals in the classroom. Suggests suitable animals and information sources to assist with animal care.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Jeff Spencer, Ivan Gaal and Curriculum Branch, Ministry of Education for Zoo Education Service

results.title: Bunny
results.creator_credit: Edwin James Lynch and Philip Ellery
results.brief_description: In this engaging short drama, Jane is convinced that her dog has killed the neighbours’ pet rabbit! In fact, the rabbit died of natural causes and was given a decent burial…. Jane deals with the problem with the naive resourcefulness of a young girl, much to the horror of her neighbours!
results.description: In this engaging short drama, Jane is convinced that her dog has killed the neighbours’ pet rabbit! In fact, the rabbit died of natural causes and was given a decent burial…. results.title: Impression of Oakleigh Technical School. . a caring place
results.brief_description: An overview of Oakleigh Technical School as seen throught the eyes of Scott, one of the students. The school is show as having a vibrant school council with an innovative, stimulating program.

results.title: Mainly monkeys. Koalas and co
results.creator_credit: Louise Jonas, Rob McCubbin and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Contains two programmes filmed at Melbourne Zoo. 1.”Mainly monkeys” shows the social behaviour of groups of monkeys and other primates that live in trees. 2.”Koalas and Co.” observes some Australian animals adapted for life in trees, such as koalas, fruit bats, tree frogs and lizards. Both programmes are non-narrated, making use of natural sound.

results.title: Arrivederci Roma
results.creator_credit: Geoffrey Wright, Richard Zotorski, Australian Film Commission and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: Marinelli (Robert Forza) is a new immigrant to Australia who experiences difficulties when confronted with the harshness of the Anglo-saxon culture during a swimming lesson. With an inability to speak English, Marinelli is plunged into the deep end of the pool and cultural survival. Directed by Geoffrey Wright known mostly for ‘Romper Stomper’ and ‘Metal Skin’. Set in the City Baths, Melbourne.

results.title: Here's to Your health: std's
results.creator_credit: Craig Reardon, Robert Grant and Victoria. Dept. of Health. Health Promotion Unit
results.brief_description: This program sets out to raise the awareness of issues regarding sexually transmitted diseases. It emphasizes the presentation of correct information and examines the transmission of STD’s and the prevention of all STD’s.

results.title: Bob Graham
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Lily Steiner and Victoria. Ministry of Education
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Cover to cover”.
Looks at the work of Bob Graham, an Australian author-illustrator. This successful illustrator has a clean, simple style which belies the depth and imagery of his work.

results.title: Dressing up in the past
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, David Ferris and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Presents Lorri Baines, National Gallery Education Officer, demonstrating what the gallery offers children in terms of the history of clothing and costume. Using two children as models she shows how eighteen century aristocrats would have dressed and the restrictions imposed by their mode of dress.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Jeff Spencer, David Ferris and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
